title: Ornithopus junceus
intro: Ornithopus junceus, commonly known as bird's foot trefoil or juncea clover, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include alleviating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The bioactive constituents of Ornithopus junceus include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ornithopus junceus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
taxonomy: Ornithopus junceus is classified in the family Fabaceae. This flowering plant belongs to the genus Ornithopus, which includes several species of burr clover.
